questions.question: Implement a stack using an array in C with functions for push, pop, and peek operations. Include overflow and underflow condition checks. Also state the time complexity of each operation.
keyPoints: Define MAX_SIZE and array structure for stack
Implement peek() to return top element without removal

questions.question: Compare the array-based and linked list-based implementations of Stack ADT in terms of memory utilization, time complexity of push and pop operations, and ease of resizing. Which implementation would you prefer and why?
keyPoints: Array: fixed size, O(1) operations, may have overflow/wastage
Linked list: dynamic size, O(1) operations, uses extra memory for pointers
Array needs rehashing for resize, linked list grows naturally
Linked list preferred for unpredictable size, array for fixed known size

questions.question: Write a complete C program to implement Stack using a singly linked list. Include functions to: (a) push an element, (b) pop an element, (c) display all elements, (d) check if stack is empty, and (e) free the entire stack. Also write a main function to demonstrate all operations.
keyPoints: Define node structure with data and next pointer
push(): create new node, insert at beginning (O(1))
pop(): remove from beginning, handle empty stack (O(1))
Complete main() demonstrating all stack operations

questions.question: Write a C program to evaluate a postfix expression using a stack. Your program should: (a) accept a postfix expression as input (e.g., '5 1 2 + 4 * + 3 -'), (b) push operands onto stack, (c) apply operators on top two elements when operator encountered, (d) display the final result. Handle binary operators +, -, *, and /.
keyPoints: Algorithm: scan postfix expression left to right
When operand: convert to integer and push onto stack
When operator: pop two operands, compute, push result
Handle errors: insufficient operands, division by zero, invalid expression
